label: The Coach
gift: Calling people up — reminding them of the power they've forgotten and the level they've outgrown.

cards.name: See the Level You've Outgrown
cards.essence: Notice the level you keep playing on that you've actually outgrown.
steps: Notice where the game feels too small or stale.
Feel the boredom or itch in your body — it's data.
Name the level you've outgrown out loud.
cards.primaryQuestion: What level am I still playing on that I've already outgrown?
cards.campaignQuestion: Where am I staying small out of habit?
cards.optimizesFor: Catching your own outgrown level before it quietly caps you.
forbiddenMoves: Mistaking comfort for fit
Pressuring yourself to level up before you see it
Calling restlessness failure
cards.remediation: Name one level you've outgrown; sit with the discomfort for a breath.

tell.working: You feel the itch and name it.
tell.performed: You declare a new level to look impressive.

contraindications: When you actually need to consolidate, not level up
cards.example: The work bores you; instead of grinding, you name the level you've outgrown.

cards.name: See the Power They've Forgotten
cards.essence: Notice the power the person in front of you has forgotten they have.
steps: Look past what they're doing to what they're capable of.
Feel where they've gone small.
Name the strength you see, specifically.
cards.primaryQuestion: Am I seeing their capacity or just their current performance?
cards.campaignQuestion: What power has this person forgotten they have?
cards.optimizesFor: Seeing people at their capacity, not their slump.
forbiddenMoves: Fixing them
Flattery (the Empty Cheerleader)
Pressuring them to perform
cards.remediation: Name one specific strength you genuinely see in them.
contraindications: When they need practical help, not a pep read
cards.example: You tell a discouraged friend the specific strength you've watched them use.

cards.name: Notice the level you've outgrown — Edge
cards.essence: In yourself: calling people up, at the edge, naming what resists.
steps: Notice what is actually here — at the edge, naming what resists.
Turn it inward: the level you've outgrown.
cards.primaryQuestion: What am I not letting myself notice here?
cards.campaignQuestion: What does this situation need someone to see?
cards.optimizesFor: Seeing what is actually there before reacting — your calling people up at the Edge level.
forbiddenMoves: Reacting before you have actually looked
Sliding into the Taskmaster — dragging instead of calling up, creating dependence.
failureModes: Blind spots you swear aren't there
cards.remediation: Name one thing you hadn't let yourself see.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Taskmaster — dragging instead of calling up, creating dependence.

cards.name: Notice the power they've forgotten they have — Edge
cards.essence: For others: calling people up, at the edge, naming what resists.
steps: Notice what is actually here — at the edge, naming what resists.
Bring it to others: the power they've forgotten they have.

cards.optimizesFor: Seeing what is actually there before reacting — your calling people up at the Edge level.
forbiddenMoves: Reacting before you have actually looked

failureModes: Blind spots you swear aren't there
cards.remediation: Name one thing you hadn't let yourself see.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Taskmaster — dragging instead of calling up, creating dependence.

cards.name: Receive your own resistance to being called up — Sense
cards.essence: In yourself: calling people up, sensing what is here.
steps: Receive it without flinching — sensing what is here.
Turn it inward: your own resistance to being called up.
cards.primaryQuestion: What am I actually feeling, underneath?
cards.campaignQuestion: What is the other person or group really reaching for?
cards.optimizesFor: Receiving the charge instead of bracing against it — your calling people up at the Sense level.
forbiddenMoves: Numbing or performing the feeling instead of feeling it
Sliding into the Taskmaster — dragging instead of calling up, creating dependence.
cards.remediation: Let it land for one breath before you respond.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Taskmaster — dragging instead of calling up, creating dependence.

cards.name: Meet Your Own Resistance
cards.essence: Let yourself feel your resistance to being called up, without obeying it.
steps: Notice the part that wants to stay where it's safe.
Feel the resistance in your body.
Thank it, and choose to move anyway.
cards.primaryQuestion: What am I protecting by refusing to level up?
cards.campaignQuestion: What resistance in me is keeping me small?
cards.optimizesFor: Moving up without bulldozing your own fear.
forbiddenMoves: Forcing past the fear
Pressuring yourself into shame
Calling caution cowardice
cards.remediation: Name the resistance; take one small step with it, not against it.

tell.working: You move with the fear, not over it.
tell.performed: You shame yourself into a sprint.

contraindications: When the resistance is wisdom, not fear
cards.example: You feel the urge to hide; you name it and send the thing anyway.

cards.name: Receive Their Resistance
cards.essence: Hear the person’s resistance to being called up without overriding it.
steps: Ask what makes the next level feel risky for them.
Feel their fear as legitimate, not an obstacle.
Reflect it back before you invite the step.
cards.primaryQuestion: Am I honoring their fear or steamrolling it?
cards.campaignQuestion: What makes leveling up feel unsafe for them?
cards.optimizesFor: Calling people up at a pace they can actually take.
forbiddenMoves: Dragging them past their no
Guilt-tripping them upward
Dismissing the fear
cards.remediation: Name their fear in their words; ask what would make one step feel safe.

tell.performed: You push and they comply resentfully.

contraindications: When they've clearly said not now
cards.example: You ask what feels scary about the bigger role before nudging them toward it.

cards.name: Receive your own resistance to being called up — Whole
cards.essence: In yourself: calling people up, integrating the whole field.
steps: Receive it without flinching — integrating the whole field.
Turn it inward: your own resistance to being called up.
cards.primaryQuestion: What am I actually feeling, underneath?
cards.campaignQuestion: What is the other person or group really reaching for?
cards.optimizesFor: Receiving the charge instead of bracing against it — your calling people up at the Whole level.

contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Taskmaster — dragging instead of calling up, creating dependence.

cards.campaignQuestion: What is the other person or group really reaching for?

cards.remediation: Let it land for one breath before you respond.

cards.name: Clear the story keeping you on a dead level — Sense
cards.essence: In yourself: calling people up, sensing what is here.
steps: Transform the charge — sensing what is here.
Turn it inward: the story keeping you on a dead level.
cards.primaryQuestion: What charge am I carrying that needs to move?
cards.campaignQuestion: What needs to be metabolized before we move together?
cards.optimizesFor: Metabolizing the charge so it stops leaking — your calling people up at the Sense level.
forbiddenMoves: Venting the charge onto someone else
Sliding into the Taskmaster — dragging instead of calling up, creating dependence.
cards.remediation: Name what you are feeling out loud before you act.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Taskmaster — dragging instead of calling up, creating dependence.

cards.name: Clear the story that keeps them small — Sense
cards.essence: For others: calling people up, sensing what is here.
steps: Transform the charge — sensing what is here.
Bring it to others: the story that keeps them small.
cards.primaryQuestion: What charge am I carrying that needs to move?
cards.campaignQuestion: What needs to be metabolized before we move together?
cards.optimizesFor: Metabolizing the charge so it stops leaking — your calling people up at the Sense level.
forbiddenMoves: Venting the charge onto someone else
Sliding into the Taskmaster — dragging instead of calling up, creating dependence.
cards.remediation: Name what you are feeling out loud before you act.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Taskmaster — dragging instead of calling up, creating dependence.

cards.name: Drop the Story Keeping You Small
cards.essence: Clear the story that keeps you on a level you've outgrown.
steps: Write the story you tell about why you can't move up.
Feel where it's fear wearing fact's clothes.
Rewrite it as the version that isn't certain.
cards.primaryQuestion: What story is keeping me on a dead level?
cards.campaignQuestion: What belief about myself is capping me here?
cards.optimizesFor: Freeing yourself from the script that caps you.
forbiddenMoves: Defending the story
Toxic positivity over it
Pressuring a breakthrough
cards.remediation: Say the story aloud, then the part you actually don't know is true.

tell.performed: You slap an affirmation over it.

contraindications: When the limit is real and worth respecting
cards.example: You name 'I'm not a leader' as a story, then test it once.

cards.name: Name the Story That Keeps Them Small
cards.essence: Surface the story keeping the other person on a level they've outgrown.
steps: Reflect the limiting story you hear them tell ('I'm just the…').
Feel for whether they're ready to question it.
Offer the question, not the answer: 'is that still true?'
cards.primaryQuestion: Am I imposing my read or surfacing theirs?
cards.campaignQuestion: What story is keeping this person from their next level?
cards.optimizesFor: Loosening the script without doing their work for them.
forbiddenMoves: Telling them who to be
Pressuring the reframe
Ripping the story away before they're ready
cards.remediation: Ask 'is that story still serving you?' and let them answer.
contraindications: When trust isn't there yet
cards.example: You gently ask a teammate if 'I'm bad at this' is still true.

cards.campaignQuestion: What needs to be metabolized before we move together?

cards.name: Build your capacity to call up without dragging — Sense
cards.essence: In yourself: calling people up, sensing what is here.
steps: Build the capacity — sensing what is here.
Turn it inward: your capacity to call up without dragging.
cards.primaryQuestion: Who do I need to become for this?
cards.campaignQuestion: What capacity does this work need us to build?
cards.optimizesFor: Building the capacity the work keeps asking for — your calling people up at the Sense level.
forbiddenMoves: Forcing growth or skipping the rep
Sliding into the Taskmaster — dragging instead of calling up, creating dependence.
cards.remediation: Pick one small rep and do it once.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Taskmaster — dragging instead of calling up, creating dependence.

cards.campaignQuestion: What capacity does this work need us to build?
cards.optimizesFor: Building the capacity the work keeps asking for — your calling people up at the Edge level.
forbiddenMoves: Forcing growth or skipping the rep
Sliding into the Taskmaster — dragging instead of calling up, creating dependence.
cards.remediation: Pick one small rep and do it once.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Taskmaster — dragging instead of calling up, creating dependence.

cards.name: Build their next level, at their pace — Edge
cards.essence: For others: calling people up, at the edge, naming what resists.
steps: Build the capacity — at the edge, naming what resists.
Bring it to others: their next level, at their pace.
cards.primaryQuestion: Who do I need to become for this?
cards.campaignQuestion: What capacity does this work need us to build?

cards.remediation: Pick one small rep and do it once.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Taskmaster — dragging instead of calling up, creating dependence.

cards.name: Coach Yourself Without Dragging
cards.essence: Build the capacity to call yourself up steadily, without self-tyranny.
steps: Set one stretch that's just past comfortable.
Feel the difference between challenge and self-punishment.
Practice the rep; rest is part of it.
cards.primaryQuestion: Am I calling myself up or just driving myself?
cards.campaignQuestion: What sustainable practice grows me without burning me?
cards.optimizesFor: Self-development that lasts, not a crash diet of willpower.
forbiddenMoves: Becoming your own Taskmaster
No-rest grinding
Pressuring yourself past sense
cards.remediation: Pick one stretch and one rest; do both.

tell.performed: You flog yourself and call it growth.

contraindications: When you need recovery more than a stretch
cards.example: You set a stretch goal and schedule the rest that makes it sustainable.

cards.name: Grow Them at Their Pace
cards.essence: Steward another's growth over time without creating dependence.
steps: Find the next stretch that's theirs, not yours.
Feel where you're tempted to do it for them.
Hand back the rep; let them carry it.
cards.primaryQuestion: Am I building their capacity or my indispensability?
cards.campaignQuestion: What's their next right stretch, at their pace?
cards.optimizesFor: People who can do it without you.
forbiddenMoves: Rescuing
Pressuring their pace
Making them depend on you
cards.remediation: Name one thing you'll stop doing for them this week.

tell.working: They need you less over time.

contraindications: When they genuinely need you to step in now
cards.example: You let a mentee struggle through the task you could finish in a minute.

cards.name: Call yourself up to the next level — Sense
cards.essence: In yourself: calling people up, sensing what is here.
steps: Take the concrete act — sensing what is here.
Turn it inward: yourself up to the next level.
cards.primaryQuestion: What is the one act I keep avoiding?
cards.campaignQuestion: What concrete act would actually help right now?
cards.optimizesFor: Taking the one concrete act that moves things — your calling people up at the Sense level.
forbiddenMoves: Hinting or prepping forever instead of acting
Sliding into the Taskmaster — dragging instead of calling up, creating dependence.
failureModes: The move that never quite happens
cards.remediation: Do the smallest real version today.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Taskmaster — dragging instead of calling up, creating dependence.

cards.name: Call them up — name the level and the why — Sense
cards.essence: For others: calling people up, sensing what is here.
steps: Take the concrete act — sensing what is here.

failureModes: The move that never quite happens
cards.remediation: Do the smallest real version today.
contraindications: Overusing this becomes the Taskmaster — dragging instead of calling up, creating dependence.

cards.name: Call Yourself Up
cards.essence: Name the level you’re stepping to, and step — out loud, on the record.
steps: Name the next level specifically.
Feel the fear and commit anyway.
Take the first concrete action toward it today.
cards.primaryQuestion: What level am I committing to, and what's the first move?
cards.campaignQuestion: What's the step up I keep deferring?
cards.optimizesFor: Crossing into the next level instead of admiring it.
forbiddenMoves: Declaring without doing
Waiting to feel ready
Pressuring a leap with no footing
cards.remediation: Take the smallest real action toward the level today.

tell.performed: You announce the level and stall.

contraindications: When you need a plan before a leap
cards.example: You commit to the harder role and send one email that starts it.

cards.name: Call Them Up
cards.essence: Name the level you see them ready for, and the why — then invite the step.
steps: Name the level you see them ready for, specifically.
Say why you believe it — name the evidence you’ve felt, not flattery.
Invite one concrete step — and let them choose.
cards.primaryQuestion: Am I calling them up or pushing them where I want them?
cards.campaignQuestion: What level is this person ready for that no one has named?
cards.optimizesFor: The moment someone steps up because you named what they couldn't see.
forbiddenMoves: Flattery without a step
Pressuring the yes
Naming a level for your benefit
cards.remediation: Pair the belief with one concrete, optional next step.

tell.working: They step up of their own will.

contraindications: When they haven't asked and trust is thin
cards.example: 'You're ready to lead this — here's why. Want to take the first piece?'

cards.name: Become Someone Who Calls Up
cards.essence: Integrate the shift: become a person who levels up and brings themselves whole.
steps: Name who you're becoming by taking this level.
Feel whether it’s integration or just ambition.
Carry the part of you that was scared; don't leave it behind.
cards.primaryQuestion: Who am I becoming by calling myself up?
cards.campaignQuestion: What's the durable identity shift here, not just the win?
cards.optimizesFor: Growth that integrates, not a new mask.
forbiddenMoves: Leaving your scared parts behind
Performing the upgraded self
Pressuring an identity
cards.remediation: Finish: 'I'm becoming someone who can ____ and still ____.'

tell.working: The new level feels like you.

contraindications: When you're mid-change and naming it freezes it
cards.example: You name the leader you're becoming and keep the tender part along.

cards.name: Leave Them More Capable
cards.essence: Coach so that, when you're gone, they're stronger and freer — not dependent.
steps: Aim for them to outgrow needing you.
Feel where you're holding on for your own sake.
Hand over what they can now carry; celebrate it.
cards.primaryQuestion: Will they be more capable after me, or more dependent?
cards.campaignQuestion: What capacity do I want to leave behind in them?
cards.optimizesFor: People who carry it without you — the Coach's win.
forbiddenMoves: Making yourself indispensable
Hoarding the credit
Guilt-binding them to you
cards.remediation: Name one responsibility to fully hand over now.
contraindications: When they still genuinely need scaffolding
cards.example: You hand a mentee the thing you've always done, and step back.
